title: MongoDB Authentication failed.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Can't run mongodb with authentication enabled while it works in docker

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
podman pull docker.io/mongo
podman run -d --name tmongo -e MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME=mongoadmin -e MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD=secret mongo
podman exec -it tmongo bash
mongo -u monogoadmin -p msecret --authenticationDatabase admin
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
❯ podman exec -it tmongo bash                                                                                    
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
root@4050da86b554:/# mongo -u monogoadmin -p secret --authenticationDatabase admin
MongoDB shell version v5.0.3
connecting to: mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/?authSource=admin&compressors=disabled&gssapiServiceName=mongodb
Error: Authentication failed. :
connect@src/mongo/shell/mongo.js:372:17
@(connect):2:6
exception: connect failed
exiting with code 1
root@4050da86b554:/# 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
same as docker. Just replace podman in above commands with `sudo docker`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

using rootless podman.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.17.1
Git Commit:   a6493ae6904a5b7dde2395a374cb77fc45c8a3bc
Built:        Wed Oct 20 22:57:47 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical host
ArchLinux kernel 5.14.16-zen1-1-zen
with
`export DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=;` cause of this:- https://bbs.archlinux.org/viewtopic.php?id=253966

comments.body: The passwords in your example don't seem to match - `-p msecret` vs `-e MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD=secret`?

> The passwords in your example don't seem to match - `-p msecret` vs `-e MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD=secret`?

sorry, copy pasting mistake somehow. edited
Does it work with rootfull Podman?  Does it work with rootless podman run --privileged
extremely sorry. this was caused by old volumes which I was using. I did not used volumes with docker thats why it was working. in reproducible example, I was using wrong credentials.
